title: Magnitude pitches coding agents on open models at lower cost
friendlyTitle: Magnitude wants to make coding agents cheaper
summary: Magnitude launched as a coding agent that runs on open models, using smart model routing to split work between a leader model and lower-cost specialist models. The company claims pass-through API pricing, free starter credits, and large cost savings versus closed-model coding agents.
friendlySummary: Magnitude is a coding agent that uses open models and routes different parts of a task to different models. It says this can lower costs without losing performance.
whyItMatters: The economics of AI coding are becoming a product feature in their own right. If routing across open models works reliably, teams may get more predictable spend without giving up agentic coding workflows.
friendlyWhyItMatters: AI coding tools can get expensive fast. A cheaper approach could help more developers and small teams use them every day.
